content:
I am a Ph.D. Student advised by Prof. Chongjie Zhang at Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (IIIS, Tsinghua University), headed by Prof. Andrew Yao. The goal of my research is to endow robots with superhuman intelligence, which I believe can be achieved through learning from interactions using modern models.
Download my resumé .
summary: I am a Ph.D. Student advised by Prof. Chongjie Zhang at Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (IIIS, Tsinghua University), headed by Prof. Andrew Yao. The goal of my research is to endow robots with superhuman intelligence, which I believe can be achieved through learning from interactions using modern models.
title: Heng Dong (董恒)

summary: We show that diffusion models can reconstruct global states in decentralized partially observable multiagent systems, with approximation errors leading to deviations that can be bounded for convergence to the true state.
title: On Diffusion Models for Multi-Agent Partial Observability: Shared Attractors, Error Bounds, and Composite Flow

summary: We propose an LLM-based Actor-Critic algorithm that integrates actor and critic methods in the way that would utilize the merits of the actor-critic algorithm with the strengths of LLMs.
title: Enhancing Decision-Making of Large Language Models via Actor-Critic

summary: We propose to design multi-cellular robots in a coarse-to-fine manner and leverage hyperbolic embeddings for realization.
title: Leveraging Hyperbolic Embeddings for Coarse-to-Fine Robot Design

summary: We exploit the structure of the design space in robot design problems with symmetry characteristics and generate robots with high performance more efficiently.
title: Symmetry-Aware Robot Design with Structured Subgroups
Low-Rank Modular Reinforcement Learning via Muscle Synergy

summary: Use the idea of homophily to solve second-order social dilemmas.
title: Birds of a feather flock together: A close look at cooperation emergence via multi-agent rl
DOP: Off-Policy Multi-Agent Decomposed Policy Gradients
ROMA: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Emergent Roles
